medications.indication_rationale: Tetanus-prone contaminated wound with uncertain/>10-year immunisation — a booster is required; source gave an ADT booster IM in person. (Consider tetanus immunoglobulin if immunisation is inadequate/high-risk.)
For wound pain; source gave ibuprofen 400mg + paracetamol 1000mg stat.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1000mg up to QID + ibuprofen 400mg with food (e.g. TID) (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Maintains a moist wound environment for faster, scar-minimising healing; leave undisturbed for several days.

medications.drug_name: prophylactic oral antibiotics for a clean-able abrasion
medications.indication_rationale: Routine antibiotics are not indicated for a simple abrasion once cleaned; reserve for established infection.

medications.drug_name: dry gauze applied directly to the raw wound
medications.indication_rationale: Dry gauze adheres and tears new epithelium on removal — use a non-adherent moist dressing.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Gently rinse the wound with clean running water now; arrange prompt in-person care for proper irrigation/debridement and a tetanus booster; keep it covered with a non-adherent moist dressing and leave it undisturbed for several days.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Decontamination and tetanus cover prevent infection/tattooing/tetanus; moist healing speeds recovery.

patient_education_points.point: This is a graze (partial-thickness abrasion). Because it has gravel embedded and your tetanus cover is out of date, it needs proper cleaning in person and a tetanus booster — a home rinse alone won't remove the grit, which can cause infection or permanent 'tattooing'.

Rinse it now with clean water, cover it with a non-stick moist dressing (never dry gauze on the raw area), and get it seen promptly for irrigation and a tetanus shot. Keep the dressing on and the wound moist to heal faster with less scarring.

Go to ED if the wound is actually deep/gaping with exposed fat/muscle, or if it later spreads a hot red rash up/down the leg, oozes thick yellow pus, develops a red streak toward the groin, or you get a fever.

follow_up_plan.interval: prompt in-person wound care today/tomorrow; dressing review at ~5 days
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Adequate decontamination, tetanus given, healing, infection.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Deep/joint injury or spreading infection — ED.

must_include_items: Recognise the wound needs in-person irrigation/debridement of embedded grit AND a tetanus booster (uncertain/>10y)
Confirm it is superficial (exclude deep/joint injury)
acceptable_alternatives: dTpa or ADT booster; any non-adherent moist dressing; analgesia
errors_of_omission: Not arranging tetanus / not addressing embedded contamination
errors_of_commission: Dressing over embedded grit without decontamination/tetanus
Recommending dry gauze on the raw wound
Routine prophylactic antibiotics for a clean-able abrasion
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires arranging in-person irrigation/debridement + a tetanus booster, a moist dressing, and a cellulitis safety-net.
